stem: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle B is 90° and BD is an altitude of the triangle (with D on side AC). The length of AB is 20 and the length of AC is 12 greater than the length of AB. What is the value of BC/BD?
rationale: Because ∠B = 90° and BD is the altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse AC, triangles ABC and DBC are similar, giving BC/BD = AC/AB. With AB = 20 and AC = 20 + 12 = 32, this ratio equals 32/20 = 8/5.
